title: Multiparametric discovery and optimization platform
abstract: Provided herein are systems and methods for screening desirable biological variants using a high-throughput integrated system. The integrated system may be configured to input a plurality of parameters from functional studies of biological variants under applied conditions, in conjunction with integrated libraries of biological variants, and filter the inputs to produce desirable biological variants based on an input performance requirement. The system may output optimized strains, molecules, or novel molecules expected to have a desirable functional characteristic. Accordingly, the methods and systems disclosed herein enable multi-parametric studies of biological diversity and conditional diversity in systems biology.
claims: Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for generating an improved polymer variant, the method comprising (a) obtaining a dataset comprising i) sequence data comprising data on polymer variant sequences; and ii) enrichment data based on an abundance of the polymer variant sequences in a sample sorted based on the polymer variant function; and (b) applying one or more machine learning algorithms to the dataset to design an improved polymer variant. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the polymer variant sequences comprise sequences i) in an unsorted sample and ii) in the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function, wherein the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function and the unsorted sample are from a common sample. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the enrichment data are further based on an abundance of the polymer variant sequences in the unsorted sample. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the abundance of the polymer variant sequences in the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function comprises a sequence count. 5. The method of claim 3 or 4, wherein the abundance of the polymer variant sequences in the unsorted sample comprises a sequence count. 6. The method of any one of claims 3-5, wherein the enrichment data comprise an enrichment factor for a first polymer variant sequence based on i) an abundance of the first polymer variant sequence in the polymer variant sequences in the unsorted sample and ii) an abundance of the first polymer variant sequence in the polymer variant sequences in the subset of the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the enrichment factor is a functional label for the first polymer variant sequence. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the method comprises performing multiple generations of (a) and (b), wherein the improved polymer variant of (b) is used to generate the dataset of (a) in a subsequent generation of the multiple generations. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the multiple generations are at least 3 or 4 generations. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the polymer variant comprises a protein. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the protein comprises an enzyme. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the function comprises an activity of the enzyme. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the improved polymer variant comprises at least 1000 improved polymer variants. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the polymer variant sequences comprise nucleic acid sequences encoding the polymer variant. 15. The method of claim 1, further comprising, before obtaining the dataset, providing a library of cells comprising the polymer variant, wherein the polymer variant comprises polymer variants. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising generating the library of cells comprising the polymer variants. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the generating comprises providing a site saturation nucleic acid molecule library (SSL) encoding proteins with each of 20 naturally-occurring amino acids at each position. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising performing gene shuffling and error-prone polymerase chain reaction (epPCR) on the SSL to generate nucleic acid molecules encoding the polymer variants. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising transforming the nucleic acid molecules encoding the polymer variants into cells to generate the library of cells. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the cells comprise bacterial cells. 21. The method of claim 15, further comprising partitioning the library of cells to generate a plurality of partitions. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein each partition of the plurality of partitions comprises on average one or fewer cells. 23. The method of claim 21, further comprising growing the library of cells in the plurality of partitions. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising expressing the polymer variants in the library of cells in the plurality of partitions. 25. The method of claim 15, further comprising determining the polymer variant function using the library of cells comprising the polymer variants. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the polymer variants comprise proteins. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the proteins comprise enzymes. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the determining the polymer variant function comprises using an enzyme substrate and a reaction buffer. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the polymer variant function comprises enzyme activity. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the enzyme activity produces a fluorescent product. 31. The method of claim 15, further comprising sorting a subset of the library of cells comprising the polymer variants based on the polymer variant function to provide the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function and an unsorted sample. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the function is an amount of enzyme activity. 33. The method of claim 31, wherein the sorting comprises sorting the subset of the library of cells into a plurality of groups based on the function. 34. The method of claim 32, wherein the plurality of groups is at least 4 groups. 35. The method of claim 31, wherein the subset of the library of cells comprises from about 50 times to about 100 times a number of unique polymer variants expected to exist in the library of cells. 36. The method of claim 31, further comprising using the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function to determine the polymer variant sequences. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function comprises a fiducial sequence. 38. The method of claim 36, wherein using the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function to determine the polymer variant sequences comprises purifying nucleic acid molecules from the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function. 39. The method of claim 31, further comprising purifying nucleic acid molecules from the unsorted sample. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the purifying nucleic acid molecules from the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function occurs separately from the purifying nucleic acid molecules from the unsorted sample. 41. The method of claim 31, further comprising sequencing the nucleic acid molecules from the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function and the nucleic acid molecules from the unsorted sample to provide the polymer variant sequences. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein the sequencing comprises next generation sequencing. 43. The method of claim 41, further comprising using the polymer variant sequences to determine the abundance of the polymer variant sequences i) in the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function and ii) in the unsorted sample. 44. The method of claim 43, further comprising using the abundance of the polymer variant sequences i) in the sample sorted based on the polymer variant function and ii) in the unsorted sample to determine the enrichment data. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the enrichment data comprises an enrichment factor. - 116 - 46. The method of claim 1, further comprising synthesizing the improved polymer variant. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the synthesizing comprises synthesis on an oligonucleotide array. 48. The method of claim 47, further comprising assembling the improved polymer variant into a vector. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein the vector is a plasmid. 50. The method of claim 48, further comprising introducing the vector into a host cell. 51. The method of claim 8, wherein (a) of a first generation of the multiple generations comprises performing saturation mutagenesis and epPCR to provide a library of cells; wherein (a) of a second generation of the multiple generations comprises performing epPCR; and wherein (a)of a third generation of the multiple generations comprises performing epPCR. 52. The method of claim 51, wherein each generation of the multiple generations comprises multiple rounds of the sorting of. 53. A non-transitory computer readable medium comprising instructions thereon which when executed by a computer processor cause the computer processor to perform the method of any one of claims 1-14. 54. A system comprising a computer processor and the non-transitory computer readable medium of claim 53. 55. A system for processing cells comprising one or more processors and computer readable medium, the computer readable medium comprising instructions thereon that when executed by the one or more processors cause the system to: (a) partition a population of cells into a plurality of reaction volumes to yield partitioned cells; and (b) assay the partitioned cells for a phenotype to generate signals for the partitioned cells; wherein a coefficient of variation (CV) among the signals for the partitioned cells is 11% or less when the population of cells is a homogenous population of cells. 56. The system of claim 55, wherein the CV is 10% or less. 57. The system of claim 55, wherein the CV is 7% or less. 58. The system of claim 55, wherein the CV is from about 5% to about 10%. 59. The system of claim 55, wherein the homogenous population of cells comprises at least 1 million cells. - 117 - 60. The system of claim 59, wherein the homogenous population of cells comprises at least 10,000,000 cells. 61. The system of claim 55, wherein the reaction volumes comprise droplets. 62. The system of claim 55, wherein the assay comprises addition of a detection reagent to the partitioned cells. 63. The system of claim 62, wherein the assay comprises control of timing of the addition of the detection reagent to the partitioned cells. 64. The system of claim 63, wherein the detection reagent comprises an enzyme substrate. 65. The system of claim 55, wherein the assay comprises detection of the signals for the partitioned cells. 66. The system of claim 65, wherein the detection comprises optical detection. 67. The system of claim 55, wherein the assay comprises control of timing of detection signals for the partitioned cells. 68. The system of claim 55, wherein the phenotype comprises an enzyme activity. 69. The system of claim 55, wherein (a) comprises control of a volume size of each reaction volume of the plurality of reaction volumes. 70. The system of claim 55, wherein the homogenous population of cells comprises a same genetic material. 71. The system of claim 55, wherein the homogenous population of cells comprises a same gene of interest with a same sequence. 72. A method for generating an improved cell comprising (a) generating a library of mutagenized cells; (b) partitioning the library of mutagenized cells into a plurality of partitions to yield partitioned mutagenized cells; (c) screening the partitioned mutagenized cells for a phenotype; (d) based on the screening, selecting an improved cell from the partitioned mutagenized cells using a threshold gate; (e) using a feedback controller (i) in (b) to maintain a frequency of generation of the plurality of partitions or (ii) in (d) to regulate the threshold gate; and (f) using the improved cell to repeat steps (a)-(d). 73. The method of claim 72, wherein the method comprises using the feedback controller to maintain a frequency of generation of the plurality of partitions. 74. The method of claim 73, wherein the plurality of partitions comprise a plurality of droplets. - 118 - 75. The method of claim 74, wherein the feedback controller adjusts a flow rate to maintain a frequency of generation of the plurality of droplets. 76. The method of claim 72, wherein the method comprises using the feedback controller to regulate the threshold gate. 77. The method of claim 76, wherein the feedback controller raises or lowers the threshold gate. 78. The method of claim 76, wherein the feedback controller regulates the threshold gate to select a target percentage of the partitioned mutagenized cells. 79. A computer readable medium comprising instruction thereon that when executed by a computer processor cause the computer processor to perform steps (b)-(e) of claim 72. 80. A system comprising a computer processor and the computer readable medium of claim 79. - 119 -
